description:
Second concurrency epic, follow-up to fz-ul4.11 (heap + GC + CPS codegen). Builds Erlang-style processes, cooperative scheduler, mailbox + send/receive, per-process independent GC, and transferable arenas for the zero-copy send fast path.

## Design context (decisions captured in planning, do not relitigate)

- ONE shared address space across all processes — NOT BEAM-style separate spaces. Pointers are valid across process boundaries.
- Per-process heap chosen specifically for BOUNDED GC PAUSES. Each process's heap is GC'd independently; other processes keep running.
- "Gift" mechanism: when runtime can prove (statically OR dynamically) that a value's reachability is owned solely by the sender, transfer arena ownership to receiver. O(1) instead of deep copy. Closes BEAM's biggest performance hole.
- Static path: linear/uniqueness types (typer arc, separate ticket later); arena-tagged allocation when typer can prove uniqueness.
- Dynamic path: runtime walk-and-decide; if reachability is closed within a sender-owned arena, transfer; else deep copy fallback (correct under all conditions).
- Cooperative scheduler, single OS thread for v1. Multi-thread later.
- CPS codegen from fz-ul4.11 makes this trivial: a process IS a continuation pointer; switching processes is updating which continuation the trampoline drives next. The .19.1 "fiber model" question (corosensei? hand-rolled asm?) DISAPPEARS — there's no native stack to manage.

## Children

- .19.1 Process / scheduler infrastructure (process = continuation ptr)
- .19.2 spawn / self / pid (pid as a struct schema)
- .19.3 Mailbox + send / receive (copy fallback only)
- .19.4 Per-process independent GC (verification-heavy given .11 design)
- .19.5 Transferable arenas + zero-copy send fast path

## Out of scope (filed as follow-ups when they bite)

- Linking / monitoring / exit signals (BEAM's link/monitor)
- Multi-threaded scheduler / work-stealing
- Static uniqueness types in the typer
- Selective receive with pattern matching
title: Processes + cooperative scheduler + send/receive
